mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in low-rate environments show no significant outperformance relative to baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 4.5% signals strong labor market dynamics, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns fail to outperform baseline in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE < 4.5% or Rank IC < 0.02 over 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates expanding consumer demand, creating positive momentum in S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show no significant correlation with combined HOUST/RSAFS trends or Rank IC < 0.04 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 115 signals robust manufacturing activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns underperform baseline in 70%+ of INDPRO > 115 instances or Rank IC < 0.02 over 18-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 210 signals persistent inflation, prompting central bank tightening that depresses S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show no significant underperformance in 60%+ of CPIAUCSL > 210 instances or Rank IC > 0.03 in 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 50bp+ increase in FEDFUNDS within a 3-month window signals aggressive monetary tightening, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns fail to underperform baseline in 50%+ of tightening episodes or Rank IC > 0.03 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL indicate stagflation risks, causing S&P 500 returns to decline over 5 trading days due to policy uncertainty.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns show no significant correlation with FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL co-movements or Rank IC < 0.02 in 18-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly gain of 150k+ in payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strong labor market health, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns underperform baseline in 60%+ of PAYEMS > 150k instances or Rank IC < 0.03 in 2-year rolling windows.
